uncertain_words.reason: In 63M with DM+HTN — ACS must be excluded urgently before other differentials considered
Gulf expression — pressure sensation raises ACS concern substantially
Associated with chest pain in high-risk patient — could indicate LV dysfunction

subjective.chief_complaint: Chest pain since yesterday with pressure sensation and chest tightness — HTN and DM
history_of_present_illness: Chest pain (ألم في صدري) onset yesterday

exam_findings: URGENT: ECG, troponin, and vital signs required immediately

assessment.summary: Chest pain in high-risk patient (63M, HTN, DM) — acute coronary syndrome must be excluded urgently. Differential includes GERD, musculoskeletal, or anxiety.
unsupported_or_inferred: Cardiac aetiology inferred from risk profile — not confirmed
GERD and musculoskeletal are exclusion diagnoses

patient_instructions_draft: Do not delay — ACS risk is high in this profile

claims.text: ACS must be excluded urgently in 63M with HTN, DM, and chest pain with pressure sensation
Aspirin 300mg indicated if ACS not excluded
Pressure-type chest pain with tightness in DM patient — atypical presentations more common

dialect_coverage.note: ثقل على قلبي and ضيقة flagged as critical

evidence_support.note: Diagnosis requires ECG/troponin — cannot be supported by transcript alone

completeness.note: Radiation, diaphoresis, nausea, prior cardiac history not established

self_eval.summary: URGENT case — note quality is secondary to getting ECG/troponin. Multiple critical data points missing.

uncertainty.overall_label: Mandatory physician review — ACS not excluded
uncertainty.summary: Logprob below threshold (0.684) and 3 critical dialect-ambiguous terms on cardiac symptoms. ACS not excluded — mandatory physician review before note finalises.

claim_evaluations.note: Risk profile strongly supports ACS exclusion protocol
Standard ACS protocol — ECG must be done first
DM patients can have atypical presentations

physician_questions.title: Pain radiation
physician_questions.question: Does the chest pain (ألم في صدري) radiate to arm, jaw, or back?
physician_questions.reason: Radiation pattern is a key discriminator for ACS — absent in transcript

physician_questions.title: Diaphoresis
physician_questions.question: Is the patient diaphoretic (sweating)?
physician_questions.reason: Diaphoresis with chest pain and pressure sensation raises STEMI concern

physician_questions.title: Prior cardiac history
physician_questions.question: Does the patient have a prior history of MI, angina, or PCI/CABG?
physician_questions.reason: Prior cardiac history significantly changes pre-test probability for ACS

warnings: Mixed Gulf-Egyptian dialect — logprob below threshold on critical cardiac terms
